source_tree.short_desc: Post SP4.5-FIX-1 follow-ups closeout. 132 DIVERGED comment blocks; 156 Rc<RefCell<>> declarations; 9 Weak<RefCell<>> back-refs; 5 try_borrow re-entrancy defenses; 11 distinct pending_* queues; 6 SchedOp variants with 9 production call sites and 5 drain sites.

evidence_kind.comment-tag: all-caps prefix in a source comment (DIVERGED: / RUST_ONLY: / IDIOM: / UPSTREAM-GAP: / BLOCKED: / TODO:)
evidence_kind.doc-prose: passage in docs/superpowers/ making a classification claim
evidence_kind.structural-pattern: code pattern with no annotation (field type, queue+drain, try_borrow, etc.)

marker_tag.DIVERGED: observable/structural departure flagged by port author
marker_tag.RUST_ONLY: Rust-only code with no C++ counterpart
marker_tag.UPSTREAM-GAP: C++ itself is a no-op / incomplete; Rust mirrors

claimed_classification.forced: port author claims C++ shape impossible in Rust or required deps
claimed_classification.preserved: port author claims C++ design intent preserved
claimed_classification.rust-only: port author claims no C++ equivalent exists

observability.observable-surface: on a path goldens or user observation cover (timing, signals, focus, pixel output)

verdict.forced: Port Ideology forced — C++ shape literally impossible given Rust + wgpu/winit
verdict.smuggled-idiom: claimed forced/idiom, but forced-ness is downstream of an earlier unforced Rust-first choice; the first-order choice was itself idiom and the cascade is a consequence, not a necessity
verdict.rust-only-legitimate: genuinely has no C++ analogue (test aids, debug traces, embedded assets)
verdict.upstream-gap: C++ itself is a no-op / incomplete; Rust mirrors faithfully

confidence.high: verified against C++ source or cross-referenced doc that cites C++
confidence.medium: plausible from surrounding context without direct C++ read

scale.method: one method or small method group

summary.rollup_note: Verdict/evidence/claimed counts are verifiable by JSON parsing; see the Python one-liner at the bottom of the companion ledger. Rolling them by hand invites desync after edits.
summary.aggregate_note: Not all 132 DIVERGED blocks are individually entered; trivial method-rename classes (emColor Copy-mutators, emArray/emList overload splits, emAvl iterator shapes) are rolled up into aggregate entries to keep the doc scannable. Ownership / re-entrancy / structural entries are individually listed.
summary.audit_note: 2026-04-19 audit: 16/17 comment-tag entries verified against the code they cite. 1 non-match: E013 (emViewPort.rs:40) claimed 'Rc<RefCell> cycles require an alternative' but the same struct uses Weak<RefCell<emWindow>> on line 60 — Rc cycles are not the obstacle. Real cause is RefCell re-entrancy under SetGeometry/SwapViewPorts. Comment rewritten; E013 entry cleared per audit instruction. B02 bucket updated.

buckets.name: scheduler re-entrant borrow
buckets.definition: C++ reaches emScheduler re-entrantly from inside emEngine::Cycle via raw pointer; Rust's Rc<RefCell<EngineScheduler>> with DoTimeSlice holding borrow_mut() for the whole slice forbids it. Root is the Rc<RefCell<>> ownership choice. 12 mechanisms downstream.

buckets.name: parent back-references
buckets.definition: C++ uses raw-pointer back-refs from child to parent (emViewPort -> emWindow, emPanel -> emView, engines -> view). Rust Rc<RefCell<>> cycles require Weak. Entry E014 is the canonical shape. E015 (animator-forward location) is flagged unclear pending deeper review.
buckets.note: E013 (emViewPort home geometry fields) was initially placed here but audit 2026-04-19 found the comment rationale wrong — Rc<RefCell> cycles are not the obstacle (Weak handles them, as shown by E014). Real reason: RefCell re-entrancy under SetGeometry/SwapViewPorts. That makes E013 a B01-sibling, not B02. Code comment corrected; JSON entry cleared to match user audit instruction.

buckets.name: C++ class inheritance collapse
buckets.definition: C++ uses inheritance (emView : emContext; emPanel : emEngine; emScreen : emWindow; emFileModel base class; emErrorPanel : emPanel). Rust has no inheritance. Resolutions vary per case: composition, trait, adapter object, Option<backend>, or flattening.

buckets.name: function/method overloading
buckets.definition: Rust has no name overloading. Suffix renames (VisitByIdentityBare, VisitFullsizedByIdentity, SetGoalCoords), split method names (InsertAtBeg_one / Add_one / etc.), or collapse-to-single-method (RawVisit, RawZoomOut, RecurseInput).

buckets.name: operators, conversions, and Copy semantics
buckets.definition: C++ implicit operator overloads, operator[], emColor mutating setters. Rust requires explicit methods (GetPacked instead of operator u32), Copy-returns-new-value (SetRed -> returns copy), ToString renamed (trait conflict).

buckets.name: pointer identity vs value equality; iterator semantics
buckets.definition: C++ containers use pointer identity for search/iteration; C++ iterators auto-adjust under mutation. Rust uses value equality (emList GetIndexOf by PartialEq) and explicit cursors that return None on removal. emList O(1) splice -> O(n) Vec ops.

buckets.name: emCrossPtr / auto-nullifying cross-references
buckets.definition: C++ emCrossPtr auto-nulls when target is destroyed; used for OverwriteDialog, PanelPointerCache, dialog-panel wiring. Rust replaces with Weak<RefCell<>> (for target-owned types) or Option<T> (for caller-owned types) with manual null-checks. emBorder's PanelPointerCache omitted entirely because emBorder is not a panel in the Rust port.

buckets.name: signal/callback wiring (GetXxxSignal vs callback field)
buckets.definition: C++ widgets expose GetCheckSignal returning an emSignal; callers wire via ListenSignal. Rust collapses to an Option<Box<dyn FnMut(..)>> callback field (emCheckButton::on_check). Observably: the signal object is absent; the write side of the event hub is inline-dispatched. This is a structural change on the observable signal-wiring surface.

buckets.name: absent subsystems (emRec / emModel / emRef / async plugins)
buckets.definition: emRec-backed field system not ported (emCoreConfig::VisitSpeed flattened to f64 + const); emModel/emRef collapsed to Rc<RefCell<>> throughout; async emImageFileModel plugin system replaced by sync load_image_from_file stub. emFpPlugin returns Box<dyn PanelBehavior> instead of emPanel*.

buckets.name: wgpu/winit forced divergences
buckets.definition: C++ targets X11/GL; Rust targets wgpu/winit. MaterializedSurface has no C++ analogue. OsSurface Pending/Materialized two-state enum with back-channel materialization forced by winit's async surface lifecycle (see w3-popup-architecture spec). emGUIFramework DeferredAction queue for cross-closure window mutations. These are the only genuinely-forced divergences by dependency constraint; classified forced with high confidence.

location.quote: IDIOM: C++ calls Scheduler.X(...) inline during Update because its scheduler has no aliasing restrictions. Rust's RefCell<EngineScheduler> forbids inner borrows while DoTimeSlice holds the outer borrow; deferral restores inline semantics within the same time slice without violating borrow rules.

entries.claimed_rationale: RefCell forbids inner borrows while outer borrow held; deferral preserves inline semantics.

inference.reasoning: The only IDIOM: tag in the entire codebase. Tagged 'IDIOM' but the SP4 design spec itself admits the RefCell choice is what makes deferral necessary. The forced-ness of deferral is second-order; the first-order choice (wrap EngineScheduler in Rc<RefCell<>>) was never evaluated as idiom-vs-forced. CLAUDE.md line 47 ('Rc/RefCell shared state') sanctioned the first-order choice as project-wide default — which is itself an unexamined idiom adaptation.
inference.confidence_basis: Cross-referenced scheduler-refcell-workaround-ledger.md §1.3 and §6.1; SP4 spec 2026-04-18-emview-sp4-update-engine-routing-design.md:35 explicitly says 'idiom adaptation forced by RefCell<EngineScheduler>' which is the smuggling pattern verbatim.

location.quote: pub scheduler: Rc<RefCell<EngineScheduler>>,

entries.cpp_reference: C++ emScheduler is reached by raw pointer from emEngine; emGUIFramework owns it directly

inference.reasoning: This is the first-order choice. Unannotated, uncommented, no DIVERGED: tag, no IDIOM: tag. Introduced in the very first phase-4/5 commit (2026-03-04) before the Port Ideology framework existed and before any session recognized the downstream cost. All 12 mechanisms in B01 are consequences of this one line. Alternatives (event loop owns &mut EngineScheduler and threads it through Cycle via EngineCtx, or interior sharding) preserve C++ semantics and are implementable in Rust; therefore not forced.
inference.confidence_basis: Provenance: git log shows 78c113b 2026-03-04 'Implement Phases 4-5' as first-landing commit. Scheduler-refcell-workaround-ledger.md §6 enumerates three concrete alternatives that preserve C++ semantics.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: pub(crate) enum SchedOp { Fire(SignalId), WakeUp(EngineId), Connect(SignalId, EngineId), Disconnect(SignalId, EngineId), RemoveSignal(SignalId), RemoveEngine(EngineId) }

entries.cpp_reference: No C++ equivalent; C++ calls Scheduler methods inline from Cycle.
entries.claimed_rationale: SP4 spec classifies the whole deferral machinery as 'idiom adaptation forced by RefCell<EngineScheduler>'.

inference.reasoning: A six-variant enum with 9 production call sites and 5 drain sites exists because E002 made inline scheduler access impossible. C++ has no equivalent of this enum, no deferral queue, no drain points. Calling this 'idiom' and 'below-surface' is defensible per-entry but collapses under scrutiny: the drain timing affects observable what-fires-when within and across slices — the very thing the Port Ideology observability rule protects.
inference.confidence_basis: Ledger §2.1, §2.3, §2.8 enumerate the 12 mechanisms; cross-referenced the SchedOp definition and drain sites in this harvest (STRUCTURAL section).

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: pub(crate) pending_sched_ops: Vec<SchedOp>,

entries.cpp_reference: No C++ equivalent; inline scheduler access in C++ needs no queue.

inference.reasoning: Storage for E003. Drained at 5 sites (UpdateEngineClass::Cycle, App::about_to_wait, emSubViewPanel::Cycle, two PanelTree settle paths). Each drain point is its own observability commitment.
inference.confidence_basis: Harvest STRUCTURAL section: 5 drain sites confirmed.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ shares the parent emContext's scheduler via context-chain lookup (emContext::GetScheduler). Rust emSubViewPanel owns a nested PanelTree and emView; EngineCtx::tree is singular, so a single scheduler cannot cycle engines across two trees. The forced concession: this sub-view gets its own EngineScheduler.

entries.claimed_rationale: EngineCtx::tree is singular; nested PanelTree requires a second scheduler.

inference.reasoning: Claim 'forced' is precisely the second-order smuggle. Forced only given E002 and given EngineCtx::tree being singular. Both are first-order Rust choices, not constraints of Rust the language. C++ runs one scheduler per process and traverses nested trees transparently via emContext chain; with either rearchitecture option (A: event-loop-owned &mut scheduler threaded through Cycle, or C: unified EngineCtx covering multiple trees via emContext), a per-sub-view scheduler is unnecessary.
inference.confidence_basis: SP8 spec 2026-04-19-emview-sp8-subview-settlement-design.md:25 labels 'forced divergence' but ledger §2.7 explicitly notes 'unifiable only via SP7 emContext threading' — acknowledging it is not a language-forced divergence.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ emView::NoticeList is an intrusive ring on emView (emView.h:576); Rust keeps the ring fields on PanelTree to avoid RefCell re-entrancy (the view is mutably borrowed during dispatch, so callers inside callbacks cannot re-borrow it to append to a view-owned ring). Ring storage location is *forced*; dispatch driver (per-view, ...

entries.claimed_rationale: RefCell re-entrancy prevents view-owned ring.

inference.reasoning: Ring relocated off emView because emView lives in Rc<RefCell<>> and is borrowed_mut during dispatch. If emView were not behind RefCell at dispatch time (e.g., scheduler-threaded &mut), the ring could live on emView per C++. Same root as E002.
inference.confidence_basis: Comment itself names RefCell as the cause. Closeout §8.1 item 12 cross-references.

location.quote: pub(crate) fn queue_or_apply_sched_op(&mut self, op: SchedOp) { match scheduler.try_borrow_mut() { Ok(mut s) => op.apply_to(&mut s), Err(_) => self.pending_sched_ops.push(op) } }

inference.reasoning: The conditional dispatch is the deferral mechanism's entry point. 8 production call sites in emView + 1 in emPanelCtx + 1 in emPanelTree (SchedOp::RemoveEngine). The very existence of this dispatch is the observable signature of the RefCell cascade.
inference.confidence_basis: Harvest STRUCTURAL section counts call sites.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: register_engine_for uses try_borrow on view and try_borrow_mut on sched; on contention returns silently. register_pending_engines() sweeps post-DoTimeSlice to catch up deferred panels.

entries.cpp_reference: C++ emPanel registers its engine via scheduler on construction; delta=0 first Cycle.

inference.reasoning: The +1 slice drift (C++ delta=0, Rust delta=1) is a direct, measurable observable consequence. Filed as SP4.5-FIX-3. Ledger §2.6 and §4.2 detail. Same-slice fix available via SchedOp::RegisterPanelEngine drained inside UpdateEngineClass::Cycle.
inference.confidence_basis: Timing measurements doc 2026-04-19-sp4-5-fix-1-timing-measurements.md confirms delta=0 vs delta=1.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: SVPUpdSlice throttle reads scheduler.try_borrow().ok() with cached-field fallback because plain borrow() panics re-entrantly inside DoTimeSlice.

inference.reasoning: Read-side re-entrancy workaround. Stale cached counter at worst (slack-tolerant), so observable impact is negligible — but the mechanism exists only because of E002.
inference.confidence_basis: Ledger §2.4.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: UpdateEngineClass::Cycle pre-computes popup-close probe via ctx.IsSignaled(close_sig) and stashes into view.close_signal_pending; Update reads the cached bool.

entries.cpp_reference: emView.cpp:1299 checks close_signal inline in Update.

inference.reasoning: 'Move scheduler access to a point where the outer borrow doesn't exist' trick. Structurally identical to the recommended FIX-2 shape. Confirms the pattern is a generalized workaround strategy.
inference.confidence_basis: Ledger §2.5.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: let (close_sig, flags_sig, focus_sig, geom_sig) = if let Some(sched) = self.scheduler.as_ref() { let mut s = sched.borrow_mut(); (s.create_signal(), s.create_signal(), s.create_signal(), s.create_signal()) }

inference.reasoning: Open latent panic — SP4.5-FIX-2. The deferral template fails here because synchronous IDs are required. Pre-allocate-at-construction fix is itself a Rust-shaped rearrangement, not a C++ port. Existence of this hazard is a consequence of E002.
inference.confidence_basis: Closeout §8.1 item 16 SP4.5-FIX-2; ledger §4.1.

entries.carry_forward_reason: deferred by Phase 1 chunks 3+4 structural halt; see 2026-04-19-phase-1-chunk-3-blocked.md

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ emPanel inherits from emEngine directly (emPanel.h:33 — class emPanel : public emEngine). In Rust, PanelBehavior trait objects are owned by PanelTree::panels and are taken during cycling so the tree can lend a PanelCtx; a PanelBehavior therefore cannot simultaneously live in the scheduler's Box<dyn emEngine> slot-map.

entries.claimed_rationale: Single inheritance and trait-object take-out-of-place prevent dual ownership.

inference.reasoning: Rust has no inheritance AND trait-object ownership is exclusive. The adapter is genuinely forced by the combination. Classifies cleanly as forced in B03 — but the adapter's *registration* path is what triggers E008's re-entrancy cascade (B01). The forced-ness of the adapter is distinct from the smuggled consequences of registering it via E002's RefCell scheduler.
inference.confidence_basis: SP4.5 spec confirms the single-inheritance + slot-map dual-ownership constraint is language-forced.

location.quote: pub(crate) window: Option<Weak<RefCell<crate::emWindow::emWindow>>>,

inference.reasoning: Weak<RefCell<>> is the canonical Rust encoding of a non-owning back-ref where the parent owns the child in Rc<RefCell<>>. Legitimate idiom given the Rc<RefCell<>> ownership choice upstream.
inference.confidence_basis: Standard Rust pattern; 9 Weak<RefCell<>> sites in codebase all fit this shape.

location.quote: DIVERGED: animator-forward location. C++ emView::Input begins with ActiveAnimator->Input(event, state) at emView.cpp:1004, because C++ emView owns the ActiveAnimator field. In the Rust port the active animator instead lives on the input-dispatch *owner* — emWindow::active_animator (see emWindow::dispatch_input...

inference.reasoning: Field location on owner-of-dispatch rather than view may be a consequence of emView's Rc<RefCell<>> + input dispatching from emWindow through &mut view_rc.borrow_mut() — same cascade. Not investigated here; flagged for B02 follow-up.
inference.confidence_basis: Comment names the dispatch-owner-vs-field-owner split but not the ownership constraint behind it.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ class emView : public emContext — Rust has no inheritance; store the context by composition. GetContext / GetRootContext accessors below delegate, giving callers the same observable access the C++ inheritance grants.

entries.claimed_rationale: Rust has no inheritance.

inference.reasoning: Rust has no class inheritance. Composition with delegating accessors is the canonical resolution.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint, not a library choice. SP7 spec 2026-04-19-emview-sp7-emcontext-threading-design.md:70 confirms.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ emFileModel base class — Rust uses trait for type erasure since emFileModel<T> is generic.

entries.claimed_rationale: Generic base class with virtual methods mapped to trait object.

inference.reasoning: Generic base class with virtual methods is structurally a trait in Rust. Standard adaptation.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ emScreen inherits from emWindow and implements this directly. In Rust, emScreen is a monitor model without a window reference. The actual implementation is on emWindow::MoveMousePointer which uses winit's set_cursor_position.

inference.reasoning: Inheritance collapse + separation of concerns. Method moved to the type that actually owns the winit window. Observable result is identical if callers route through emWindow.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: emErrorPanel IsOpaque/Paint implemented as PanelBehavior trait methods; similar for other panel types. C++ has emPanel : emEngine virtual methods; Rust has PanelBehavior trait.

inference.reasoning: Standard inheritance→trait mapping. ~30 panel types share this shape; legitimate forced divergence per site.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: DIVERGED: not a class hierarchy but a concrete struct with optional backend hooks. Rust has no dummy-base-class pattern; the 'default implementation connects to nothing' model becomes a Weak<RefCell<emWindow>> back-reference which is None for dummy instances.

entries.claimed_rationale: No dummy-base-class pattern in Rust.

inference.reasoning: Inheritance collapse. Dummy-base pattern maps to Option<backend>. Legitimate.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: Rust cannot overload by name or arity. Policies: (1) suffix rename — VisitByIdentityBare, VisitFullsizedByIdentity, SetGoalCoords, PaintBorderImage sub-rect variants; (2) split names — emList/emArray InsertAtBeg_one/Add_one/Add_fill/etc.; (3) collapse to single method — RawVisit, RawZoomOut, RecurseInput, emFileStream::TryWrite; (4) Copy-returns-new — emColor SetRed/SetGreen/etc. 132 DIVERGED blocks include at least ~40 overload-split cases across emView, emPainter, emList, emArray, emAvlTreeMap, emAvlTreeSet, emColor, emViewAnimator, emFileStream.

entries.claimed_rationale: Rust cannot overload.

inference.reasoning: No Rust encoding admits C++-style name overloading. Per-site choice of rename vs split vs collapse is idiom; the necessity of *some* resolution is forced.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: emColor Get → GetPacked (no implicit u32 conversion); emColor Set mutators → return-new-value (emColor is Copy); ToString → Display (trait conflict); emAvlTreeMap operator[] → panics instead of default-insert; emCursor Get/ToString renames. Forced by Rust lacking implicit conversions and operator-overload conflicts with std traits.

entries.claimed_rationale: Rust lacks implicit conversion operators, Copy types cannot have mutating methods, ToString conflicts with std::string::ToString trait.

inference.reasoning: Language-level. ~15 DIVERGED blocks roll up here; all legitimate.
inference.confidence_basis: Language constraint.

location.quote: emList intrusive doubly-linked → Vec<T> (O(1) splice → O(n) copies); emArray COW TuningLevel field exists-but-no-effect; emAvlTreeSet/Map Iterator auto-advance → Cursor returns None; emList GetIndexOf by PartialEq not pointer identity; emAvlTreeMap operator[] semantics changed; emPanelTree NoticeList ring: intrusive raw-pointer linkage → Option<PanelId> arena indices.

entries.claimed_rationale: C++ intrusive data structures with raw pointers don't map to Rust's Vec/BTreeMap without giving up O(1) pointer ops. Cursor semantics differ because Rust iterators have different invariants.

inference.reasoning: Container-internal shape changes are below-surface unless they affect ordering or cursor-stability semantics. Performance divergence is acknowledged (O(n) vs O(1) splice) but not observable through goldens on measured paths. NoticeList relocation is a separate matter — linked into B01 via E006.
inference.confidence_basis: Container comments explicitly label as non-observable; goldens do not test container algorithmic complexity.

location.quote: emCrossPtr auto-nullifying raw pointer → Weak<RefCell<T>> + emCrossPtrList; Set(null) overload split into Set()/Reset(); emBorder PanelPointerCache omitted entirely; emFileDialog OverwriteDialog: emCrossPtr<emDialog> (polled each Cycle) → Option<emDialog> polled by caller.

entries.claimed_rationale: Raw pointer with auto-null doesn't map to Rust; Weak is the standard adaptation.

inference.reasoning: Most cases (emCrossPtr proper → Weak<RefCell<T>>) are canonical. emFileDialog's shift from Cycle-polled CrossPtr to caller-pulled Option<emDialog> is an observable-surface change in when the dialog is seen — flagged for deeper review but the harness-level behavior matches.
inference.confidence_basis: emFileDialog comment explicitly notes the polling-model change from Cycle-poll to caller-retrieval.

location.quote: emCheckButton/emCheckBox/emRadioButton GetCheckSignal → Option<Box<dyn FnMut(bool)>> on_check callback; CheckChanged virtual → folded into callback invocation; Clicked protected virtual → private toggle().

entries.claimed_rationale: Virtual overrides + Signal-based event hub collapsed to callback fields.

inference.reasoning: The signal object (which emCore users can listen to from arbitrary code) is replaced with a single callback slot. This may alter observable wire-up patterns for downstream code that wants multiple listeners. C++ emSignal is subscribable by any engine; Rust callback-field is single-listener. Whether goldens cover this is unknown. Flagged for review.
inference.confidence_basis: Comment claims 'inherited'/'folded' without C++ behavioral comparison; the GetCheckSignal name and its use elsewhere in C++ emCore imply multi-listener capability.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ exposes this via VisitSpeed.GetMaxValue() on an emRec-typed field (emDoubleRec VisitSpeed; in emCoreConfig.h:51; bound declared by VisitSpeed(this,'VisitSpeed',1.0,0.1,10.0) in emCoreConfig.cpp:53). Rust flattens to a plain f64 field + const because the emRec-backed scalar-field infrastructure is not ported.

entries.claimed_rationale: emRec-backed scalar-field infrastructure is not ported.

inference.reasoning: Claim is 'infrastructure not ported' — i.e., the emRec runtime-typed config field system with change notifications, persistence, and constraints is absent from the Rust port. This is a genuine gap, not a language-forced divergence. Classifying as unclear: whether this is forced-by-scope (we decided not to port it) or forced-by-language depends on emRec's architecture. Large latent surface-area.
inference.confidence_basis: emRec.h is referenced but not inspected in this harvest. Gap exists per the comment; classification requires reading emRec's design.

location.quote: emRef<T> -> Rc<T> or Rc<RefCell<T>> throughout the codebase. emScreen.rs:165 Rc<RefCell<emScreen>>; emFpPlugin.rs:445 Rc<RefCell<Self>>; emCoreConfig.rs:215 Rc<RefCell<emConfigModel<Self>>>. Rust equivalents use Rc<RefCell<T>> or OnceLock as sharing.

entries.claimed_rationale: C++ emRef shared model pattern mapped to Rc<RefCell<T>>.

inference.reasoning: The 'emRef -> Rc<RefCell<>>' policy is the project-wide decision codified in CLAUDE.md line 47. It is the same first-order choice that makes E002 load-bearing for the scheduler. 156 Rc<RefCell<>> declarations in the codebase all rest on this. Whether each site could use Rc<T> (immutable) or a different sharing encoding was not evaluated per-site. This is the largest single vector for the smuggling pattern.
inference.confidence_basis: emRef.no_rs is a literal mapping note committed to the tree; CLAUDE.md line 47 codifies the default; 156 Rc<RefCell<>> sites confirm the scale.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ uses the async emImageFileModel plugin system with format dispatching to emTga/emBmp/emGif/etc. This synchronous loader handles TGA only and serves small theme border images. Full async image loading will be ported with the image app modules.

entries.claimed_rationale: Stub; full port deferred.

inference.reasoning: Deliberately-deferred functionality, not a divergence per se. 'Will be ported' language implies preserved intent. Belongs in B09 as part of the plugin-system gap.
inference.confidence_basis: Comment itself says future work.

entries.resolution_note: emImageFileModel async loading ported via LoaderEngine (Phase 5 Task 5). load_image_from_file stub removed; register() factory + LoaderEngine added.

location.quote: The test for 'forced': not 'awkward,' not 'would require refactoring' — impossible under winit's API. When a forced divergence exists, we reproduce the C++ author's intent as tightly as possible within the smallest-necessary concession.

entries.cpp_reference: winit async surface lifecycle vs C++ X11 sync surface
entries.claimed_rationale: winit surface creation is async (before-event-loop vs during-event-loop) and returns a Handle on a later tick.
entries.observability: below-surface (surface state transitions not observable through golden pixel output)

inference.reasoning: winit's API genuinely precludes the C++ create-surface-immediately shape. OsSurface::Pending/Materialized, DeferredAction queue, and pending_framework_actions are all consequences of this one external constraint. Textbook example of a genuinely forced divergence.
inference.confidence_basis: W3 spec explicitly applies the forced-test and passes. winit docs confirm.

location.quote: DIVERGED: C++ uses emX11Screen::ScreensaverUpdateTimer (59s) with XResetScreenSaver + xscreensaver-command. Rust uses thread-local state ticked from the event loop since we don't have an X11 display handle. active_count ref-counts callers so multi-window support is correct.

entries.claimed_rationale: No X11 display handle under winit.

inference.reasoning: Genuine dependency constraint: winit abstracts away X11 handle; XResetScreenSaver is unreachable. Alternative mechanism is necessary.
inference.confidence_basis: winit API surface.

location.quote: UPSTREAM-GAP: emCore ships this as a no-op; no platform backend (emX11, emWnds) overrides it. Soft-keyboard support is absent in upstream Eagle Mode.

entries.claimed_rationale: C++ is itself a no-op; Rust mirrors.

inference.reasoning: Legitimate upstream gap; nothing to fix.
inference.confidence_basis: Self-described and verifiable against emCore source.

location.quote: RUST_ONLY: rect.rs -- Consolidates C++ pattern of passing 4 separate doubles (GetLayoutX/Y/Width/Height in emPanel.h) into a typed struct. C++ has no dedicated layout rect type.

entries.claimed_rationale: Typed wrapper to avoid passing 4 loose f64s.

inference.reasoning: Pure helper; semantically equivalent to the C++ 4-double passing.
inference.confidence_basis: Trivial.

location.quote: RUST_ONLY: toolkit_images.rs -- compile-time TGA atlas extracted from C++ emBorder::TkResources (emBorder.h:321-341). C++ loads images at runtime via emGetResImage(); Rust embeds them via include_bytes!().

entries.claimed_rationale: Compile-time embedding vs runtime loading.

inference.reasoning: Deliberate: avoids runtime resource lookup on install paths that differ between C++ and Rust (.eaglemode vs .eaglemode-rs). Pixel output unaffected.
inference.confidence_basis: Cross-referenced with emInstallInfo install-path divergence.

location.quote: RUST_ONLY: fixed.rs -- Fixed-point newtype for sub-pixel rasterization. C++ uses bare int with inline shifts (emPainter.cpp:358-374). Rust wraps in a newtype to prevent mixing fixed and integer values.

entries.claimed_rationale: Type safety helper around i32 fixed-point.

inference.reasoning: Newtype wrapper; same arithmetic. Required by CLAUDE.md pixel-arithmetic rule to prevent fixed/int mixing.
inference.confidence_basis: CLAUDE.md §Port Fidelity explicitly recommends newtypes for pixel math.

location.quote: DIVERGED: (new this wave+W3+W4+SP1+SP3+SP5+SP4.5+SP8+SP7) | 14 | 1 W3 + 4 W4 + 1 SP1 + 2 SP3 + 3 SP5 + 1 SP4.5 + 1 SP8 + 1 SP7 (emView::Context: Rc<emContext> — composition replacing C++ class emView : public emContext, forced by Rust's lack of inheritance); all warranted per CLAUDE.md

entries.claimed_rationale: Running tally of DIVERGED blocks added per wave.

inference.reasoning: Self-audit lists 14 new DIVERGED blocks 'all warranted per CLAUDE.md' — but ~half are B01 downstream consequences (sub_scheduler, NoticeList relocation, SchedOp machinery, PanelCycleEngine adapter registration) that inherit their forced-ness from E002. The audit passes because each is forced *given* E002. This entry documents the meta-pattern: the running-tally governance does not detect cascading smuggle.
inference.confidence_basis: Direct cross-reference between the tally and the B01 entries in this doc.

entries.resolution_note: Phase 5 Task 3+7: all DIVERGED blocks retrofitted with forced-category tags; mislabeled idiom blocks removed. cargo xtask annotations lint enforces categories going forward.

location.quote: Item 3 (re-entrant borrow hazard across the call tree): idiom adaptation forced by RefCell<EngineScheduler>. ... Under CLAUDE.md's classification: idiom adaptation, not a divergence. Still marked at the queue definition for discoverability.

entries.claimed_rationale: RefCell<EngineScheduler> is 'Rust ownership necessity'; deferral preserves observable what-fires-when.

inference.reasoning: This is the verbatim smuggling pattern: 'idiom adaptation *forced by* RefCell<EngineScheduler>'. The phrase mixes two classifications (idiom + forced) and resolves by calling the whole thing 'idiom, not divergence'. But RefCell is the first-order idiom choice; the second-order 'forced by RefCell' is then reclassified as idiom. This closes the accountability loop without ever reopening the RefCell decision.
inference.confidence_basis: Direct quote.

location.quote: Drift from C++. Exactly one new DIVERGED block is permitted (emView::Context composition). Subagents must not introduce other DIVERGED markers. If a subagent finds a second forced divergence, it must stop and ask before adding.

entries.claimed_rationale: Plan-level governance rule.

inference.reasoning: Governance rule caps new DIVERGED markers per sub-project. The cap works against *marked* divergences but is silent about downstream consequences and about first-order idiom-adaptations that sanction future forced-divergences. The cap + IDIOM tag together form the mechanism by which E001-E011 slip through governance.
inference.confidence_basis: Plan rule quoted verbatim; observe that SP5 landed 3 new DIVERGEDs, SP4.5 1, SP8 1, SP7 1 — per-sub-project cap respected — while the aggregate B01 cascade grew unchecked.

entries.resolution_note: Phase 5 Tasks 2-4+7: cargo xtask annotations lint enforces category tags on all DIVERGED:/RUST_ONLY: blocks, replacing per-sub-project cap governance with mechanical lint enforcement.

location.quote: Critical: self.CoreConfig.borrow() must be called *before* self.VisitingVA.borrow_mut() OR the va binding must be dropped before this line — otherwise two borrows on self. Since va borrows VisitingVA (not CoreConfig), distinct RefCells mean simultaneous borrows are fine. But the borrow of self itself is split...

inference.reasoning: Plan-level hazard note: fields on emView are themselves Rc<RefCell<>> and their borrow ordering must be manually managed. Symptom of the project-wide 'everything is Rc<RefCell<>>' decision (E027). Not a bug, but cognitive tax imposed by the first-order choice that doesn't exist in C++.
inference.confidence_basis: Direct quote; confirms that even within-view field access requires borrow-ordering reasoning, which is a second-order consequence of E027's policy.

location.quote: let mut throwaway_sched_input = crate::emScheduler::EngineScheduler::new(); ... // Task 5 BLOCKED: threading a scheduler into PanelBehavior::Input requires widening the trait signature (~100+ impls).

entries.cpp_reference: emPanel::Input receives events via the same emContext-resolved scheduler as Cycle; no throwaway exists in C++.
entries.claimed_rationale: Transitional: PanelBehavior::Input trait method has no ctx/scheduler parameter in the Rust port. Threading one requires signature change across ~100+ impls — deferred to a dedicated Phase 1.76 so Phase 1.75 closeout is not blocked by a mechanical cascade. Observable impact narrow — only signal-driven reactions that fire ONLY during input dispatch are affected; goldens 237/6 unchanged across Tasks 3–6.
entries.scale: 1 throwaway site + ~100 PanelBehavior::Input impls that would need ctx threading
entries.introduced: phase-1-75 (surfaced by sub_scheduler deletion in Task 4)
entries.inference: Phase 1.75 Tasks 3/4 removed the per-sub-view scheduler but left Input dispatch synthesizing a local EngineScheduler to satisfy SchedCtx lifetimes. This is not a forced divergence — the trait signature is malleable — but is out of Phase 1.75 scope.

entries.title: emFileDialog POSITIVE overwrite-confirm end-to-end test deferred
entries.context: Task 3's livelock fix (commit d99cedea) ensures OD POSITIVE promotes overwrite_asked→overwrite_confirmed + sets outer pending_result=Ok. Mechanical-arbiter end-to-end test deferred because install_pending_top_level_headless takes no WindowId parameter and collides when two dialogs (outer + OD) both try to install headlessly. Source-read evidence exists; end-to-end test requires parameterizing headless-install with a caller-supplied WindowId.
entries.resolution_path: Phase 3.6.2 rejected the WindowKey refactor (181 WindowId sites across 18 files) after audit: no queued phase 4–5 work needs multi-headless-window infra. Landed a narrow test-only sidecar instead — `App::headless_dialog_results: HashMap<DialogId, DialogResult>` (#[cfg(test)]) + `App::read_dialog_finalized_result` helper. The OD's POSITIVE pending_action closure reads od_result through the helper; production semantics unchanged (#[cfg(not(test))] still walks dialog_windows → windows → tree). New test `save_mode_overwrite_positive_finishes_outer_dialog_via_scheduler` covers the POSITIVE branch at emFileDialog.rs lines 187-205 (C++ emFileDialog.cpp:93-96).
entries.phase_3_6_progress: Task 5 wrote scheduler-driven tests for fsb-trigger, OD-finish observation + teardown, OD-NEGATIVE, and no-signal baseline. POSITIVE path closed in Phase 3.6.2.

entries.title: emFileDialog::CheckFinish / run_file_dialog_check_finish duplication
entries.context: Task 2 installed run_file_dialog_check_finish (free fn, post-show closure path) but left emFileDialog::CheckFinish (pre-show public method) with the validation body duplicated. Two call sites encode the C++ algorithm; drift risk if C++ updates. Reviewer flagged.
entries.resolution_path: Phase 3.6.2 factored three shared helpers: `check_finish_dir_and_open` (C++ :119-163), `classify_save_overwrite` (C++ :165-185) returning a `SaveOverwriteOutcome` enum, and `build_overwrite_dialog` (C++ :186-191). Both CheckFinish (pre-show, tree-reach on self.dialog.pending) and run_file_dialog_check_finish (post-show, ctx.tree + outer_dlg) now dispatch through the shared helpers; the remaining wrapper code is strictly the pre/post-show tree-access shape plus OD subscription routing (wake_up_signals vs scheduler.connect). Behavioral convergence: CheckFinish no longer clears overwrite_confirmed when text == confirmed (C++-faithful; prior Rust-only clear was over-zealous).
entries.phase_3_6_1_progress: Task 2 shipped with duplication; dedup landed Phase 3.6.2.
